chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male patient presents with a history of myocardial infarction.
history: The patient has a history of hypertension, dyslipidaemia, and type 2 diabetes mellitus. He has a body mass index of >/=27kg/m(2) and established atherosclerotic disease. He is currently not on any medications.
exam: Vital signs: BP 140/90 mmHg, HR 80 bpm, RR 20 breaths/min. Physical examination reveals a more atherogenic lipid profile.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: elevated LDL cholesterol, decreased HDL cholesterol. Imaging: not mentioned.
correct_diagnosis: Potential benefit from semaglutide in patients with metabolic syndrome
differentials: Potential benefit from semaglutide in patients with metabolic syndrome
correct_ranking: Potential benefit from semaglutide in patients with metabolic syndrome
